name: Heap (Auto-Capture Analytics Platform)
pricing_notes: Free trial; Growth from $3,600/yr; Pro custom; Enterprise custom

auth.notes: Server-side API key for Track API

performance.notes: REST API; batch event ingestion supported

agent_readiness.notes: Auto-capture user behavior analytics (every click, form, pageview captured automatically). heapanalytics.track(app_id, identity, event, properties). Server-side Track API for backend events. Identity: heapanalytics.add_user_properties(app_id, identity, properties). Retroactive analysis: define events after the fact from captured interactions. No instrumentation needed on front-end. Mixpanel-alternative focused on auto-capture. Acquired by ContentSquare 2023.

gotchas: Python SDK mainly for server-side event enrichment (auto-capture is JS/mobile)
GDPR data deletion and compliance requires configuration
best_when: Product teams wanting auto-captured user behavior without instrumentation
Retroactive funnel analysis from automatically captured events
Server-side event augmentation via Track API
When Mixpanel's manual instrumentation overhead is too high
avoid_when: Budget-constrained startups (use PostHog or Amplitude free tier)
Privacy-first products (auto-capture collects everything by default)
summary: Heap auto-captures all user interactions (clicks, forms, pageviews) without instrumentation. Python SDK for server-side Track API: heapanalytics.track(app_id, identity, event, properties). Define events retroactively from captured data. No front-end code changes needed. Acquired by ContentSquare (2023). Expensive but reduces analytics instrumentation burden. Use PostHog or Amplitude for startup budgets.
